title: Dispatches Table and POST /dispatches Endpoint with Ephemeral API Keys

story.as_a: orchestrator agent dispatching a sub-agent to work on a specific story in a specific role
story.i_want_to: request a dispatch-scoped ephemeral API key that carries a recorded lineage path back to my own dispatch, binds to a single story and role, and expires automatically at task completion
story.so_that: sub-agents never inherit a shared long-lived credential, loopctl can reason about the full dispatch tree, and leaked keys have bounded blast radius

rationale: Loopctl's chain-of-custody currently treats every caller identically by api_key, which means sub-agents dispatched with a shared environment variable all look identical to the chain-of-custody checks. The redesigned model (chain-of-custody-v2.md section 6) replaces this with per-dispatch ephemeral keys that record their parent lineage. This story ships the table, the endpoint, and the key minting — the lineage-based self-check (US-26.2.2) and the skill update (US-26.2.3) depend on this foundation.

acceptance_criteria.description: Migration creates `dispatches` table with columns: id (uuid PK), tenant_id (uuid FK NOT NULL), parent_dispatch_id (uuid FK self-reference, nullable for root dispatches), api_key_id (uuid FK to api_keys, the ephemeral key minted for this dispatch), agent_id (uuid FK to agents), story_id (uuid FK to stories, nullable for non-story dispatches like admin ops), role (enum: :agent, :orchestrator), lineage_path (uuid[] NOT NULL — array of dispatch ids from root to self, inclusive), created_at (timestamptz NOT NULL), expires_at (timestamptz NOT NULL), revoked_at (timestamptz, nullable).
GIN index on `lineage_path` for efficient prefix-match and containment queries. Standard index on `(tenant_id, expires_at DESC)` for active-dispatch queries.

`POST /api/v1/dispatches` endpoint requires authentication. Request body: `{parent_dispatch_id (uuid, optional for root), role (string), story_id (uuid, optional), agent_id (uuid), expires_in_seconds (integer, default 3600, max 14400)}`. Returns 201 with `{dispatch: {id, lineage_path, ...}, api_key: {raw_key, role, agent_id, expires_at}, next_action: {description, learn_more, example}}`.

The root dispatch (one per tenant lifetime) is created at tenant signup (US-26.0.1) from the operator's WebAuthn ceremony. Its `parent_dispatch_id` is NULL, its `lineage_path` is `[its own id]`, and its `role` is `:user`. Subsequent dispatches descend from this root.

Lineage path computation: `NEW.lineage_path = parent.lineage_path ++ [NEW.id]`. If `parent_dispatch_id` is NULL, `lineage_path = [NEW.id]`. Enforced in the context function, backstopped by a trigger that rejects inserts with inconsistent lineage_path.

Ephemeral api_key minting: `Loopctl.Auth.generate_api_key/1` accepts an `expires_at` parameter and creates a key that the existing auth pipeline treats as revoked after `expires_at`. The api_key is created atomically with the dispatch row in a single Ecto.Multi.

Oban worker `Loopctl.Workers.RevokeExpiredDispatchesWorker` runs every minute and revokes dispatches (and their associated api_keys) past their `expires_at`. Setting `revoked_at` on the dispatch cascades to revoking the api_key atomically.

Revoking a parent dispatch cascades to all descendants: `Loopctl.Dispatches.revoke/2` sets `revoked_at` on the target AND all dispatches whose `lineage_path @> ARRAY[target.id]` (GIN-indexed containment query). Happens in a single Ecto.Multi.
`GET /api/v1/dispatches/:id` returns a dispatch record (without the raw api_key — only the id and prefix). Requires authentication; tenant-scoped.

`GET /api/v1/dispatches?tenant_id=...&role=...&active_only=true` lists dispatches filtered by role and active status. Paginated. Useful for the operator dashboard and the rotating verifier selection in US-26.2.2.

The dispatch creation request is audit-logged via the audit chain from US-26.1.1: action `dispatch_created`, actor_lineage set to the caller's dispatch lineage, payload includes the new lineage_path, role, and expires_at.

Tenant isolation: cannot create a dispatch with a parent_dispatch_id or agent_id belonging to another tenant. Cross-tenant attempts return 404 (not 403) consistent with loopctl's existing isolation convention.

OpenAPI spec updates: new `DispatchRequest`, `DispatchResponse`, `DispatchListResponse` schemas. Operation specs for the three new endpoints. CHANGELOG entry. A system article stub at slug `dispatch-lineage` is reserved (content authored in US-26.3.3).

test_cases.name: Root dispatch is created at tenant signup with NULL parent and lineage_path = [self]
steps: Query dispatches for the new tenant
expected_results: lineage_path has length 1 and contains the dispatch's own id

test_cases.name: Child dispatch inherits and extends the parent's lineage_path
preconditions: Authenticated caller holds the root dispatch's api_key
steps: POST /dispatches with parent_dispatch_id = root.id, role: agent, story_id: some story
expected_results: dispatch.expires_at is default 1 hour in the future

test_cases.name: Revoking parent dispatch cascades to all descendants
preconditions: Dispatch tree: root → A → A1, A → A2
expected_results: A, A1, A2 all have revoked_at set
Associated api_keys for A, A1, A2 are revoked

test_cases.name: Expired dispatches are revoked by the worker
Ephemeral api_key is not accepted after expiry
steps: Attempt GET /api/v1/projects using the expired raw_key

test_cases.name: Tenant isolation: cross-tenant parent_dispatch_id returns 404
steps: POST /dispatches with parent_dispatch_id = tenant_a.root.id as tenant_b
expected_results: No dispatch is created in either tenant

test_cases.name: Dispatch creation writes an audit chain entry
expected_results: audit_chain has a new entry with action = "dispatch_created"
actor_lineage matches the caller's dispatch lineage
Payload includes the new dispatch_id, role, and expires_at

technical_notes: Oban cron entry: `{"* * * * *", RevokeExpiredDispatchesWorker}` to run every minute.
The parent_dispatch_id self-reference FK uses ON DELETE RESTRICT (revoking, not deleting).

Loopctl.Dispatches.revoke/2 uses a CTE-based UPDATE to revoke all descendants in one query: `UPDATE dispatches SET revoked_at = now() WHERE id = ANY(SELECT id FROM dispatches WHERE lineage_path @> ARRAY[$1]::uuid[])`.
Authentication of ephemeral keys: the existing ResolveApiKey plug already checks expires_at — verify and extend if needed. The key becomes invalid automatically.
The raw_key is returned ONLY at creation time. Subsequent GETs return only the id and prefix.
The tenant signup ceremony from US-26.0.1/US-26.0.2 is extended to create the root dispatch as part of the same Ecto.Multi.
